meaning: adj.底比斯的,底比斯人的n.底比斯人,底比斯
examples: The historical background for theban deserves to be examined.
He called the Theban and Corinthian shepherds to prove the truth.
An Athenian and a Theban were on the road together, and passed the time in conversation, as is the way of travelers.
He fought first the Theban Greeks, then the Persians, then the Bactrians and other Central Asian peoples, and finally the Indian peoples of the Punjab.
